---
title: "ggnomics: Expression Heatmap"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
---

```{python}
import importlib.util, os
spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("mock_data", os.path.join(os.getcwd(), "examples", "00_mock_data.py"))
mock_data = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
spec.loader.exec_module(mock_data)

from ggnomics import plot_heatmap

df    = mock_data.make_mock_df(n_cells=500)
adata = mock_data.make_mock_anndata(n_cells=500)
sce   = mock_data.make_mock_sce(n_cells=500)

# Select marker genes
MARKERS = [f"Gene{i+1:04d}" for i in range(0, 50, 2)][:15]
```

## Overview

`plot_heatmap` produces a gene-expression heatmap using `geom_tile`.  Rows
(genes) and columns (cells or groups) can be reordered by hierarchical
clustering.  When `group_by` is supplied, per-group mean expression is
plotted instead of per-cell values — much faster for large datasets.

---

## 1 — Per-group heatmap (grouped mean)

```{python}
p = plot_heatmap(df, features=MARKERS, group_by="cluster",
                 title="Grouped heatmap — Z-scored")
p.save("examples/img/04a_heatmap_grouped.png", width=7, height=6, dpi=120)
p
```

## 2 — Cluster both rows and columns

```{python}
p = plot_heatmap(df, features=MARKERS, group_by="cluster",
                 cluster_rows=True, cluster_cols=True,
                 title="Heatmap — rows + cols clustered")
p.save("examples/img/04b_heatmap_clustered.png", width=7, height=6, dpi=120)
p
```

## 3 — No row clustering

```{python}
p = plot_heatmap(df, features=MARKERS, group_by="cluster",
                 cluster_rows=False, cluster_cols=False,
                 title="Heatmap — input order")
p.save("examples/img/04c_heatmap_noclust.png", width=7, height=6, dpi=120)
p
```

## 4 — Raw (unscaled) expression

```{python}
p = plot_heatmap(df, features=MARKERS, group_by="cluster",
                 scale=False, palette="Blues",
                 title="Heatmap — raw expression")
p.save("examples/img/04d_heatmap_raw.png", width=7, height=6, dpi=120)
p
```

## 5 — AnnData (logcounts layer)

```{python}
p = plot_heatmap(adata, features=MARKERS, group_by="cluster",
                 layer="logcounts", title="AnnData heatmap — logcounts")
p.save("examples/img/04e_heatmap_adata.png", width=7, height=6, dpi=120)
p
```

## 6 — SCE

```{python}
p = plot_heatmap(sce, features=MARKERS, group_by="cluster",
                 layer="logcounts", title="SCE heatmap")
p.save("examples/img/04f_heatmap_sce.png", width=7, height=6, dpi=120)
p
```

---

## Key parameters

| Parameter | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `features` | List of gene names (rows) |
| `group_by` | Obs column; if supplied, plot group means |
| `scale` | Z-score per gene (row) |
| `cluster_rows` | Hierarchically reorder rows |
| `cluster_cols` | Hierarchically reorder columns |
| `palette` | Matplotlib diverging colormap (default `"RdBu_r"`) |
| `show_colnames` | Render column axis tick labels |
